translated: Abraham was the father of Isaac. Isaac was the father of Jacob. Jacob was the father of Judah and his brothers. Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah, whose mother was Tamar. David was the father of Solomon, whose mother had been Uriah's wife. Solomon was the father of Rehoboam. Rehoboam was the father of Abijah. Abijah was the father of Asa. Asa was the father of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat was the father of Jehoram. Jehoram was the father of Uzziah. Uzziah was the father of Jotham. Jotham was the father of Ahaz. Josiah was the father of Jeconiah and his brothers at the time of the exile to Babylon. After the exile to Babylon: Jeconiah was the father of Shealtiel. Shealtiel was the father of Zerubbabel. Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud. Abiud was the father of Eliakim. Eliakim was the father of Azor. Azor was the father of Zadok. Zadok was the father of Akim. Akim was the father of Eliud. Eliud was the father of Eleazar. Eleazar was the father of Matthan. Matthan was the father of Jacob. Jacob was the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary, and Mary was the mother of Jesus who is called the Messiah. So all the generations from Abraham to David were fourteen generations, from David to the exile to Babylon fourteen generations, and from the exile to the Messiah fourteen generations.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: God created the heavens and the earth. The earth was formless and empty, and darkness covered the deep waters. And the Spirit of God was hovering over the surface of the waters.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: When the white man came to our land, he had the Bible and we had the land; now, we have the Bible and he has the land.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham was the father of Isaac, and Isaac the father of Jacob, and Jacob the father of Judah and his brothers.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: When the brothers saw that their father loved Joseph more than all his brothers, they hated him and could not speak peacefully to him.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: Jacob was a very honest man. He told the Unimboto people the truth.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: God loves those who love him, and he loves those who love God too. He does not love those who do not love him.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: If you want to go to the farm, you must go with a cutlass, if you want to go to the forest, you must go with a cutlass, if you want to go to the river to fetch water, you must go with a cutlass, because of snakes and other harmful things.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham was the father of Isaac, and Isaac the father of Jacob, and Jacob the father of Judah and his brothers.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: And it was because of this that they hated him and could not speak peacefully to him, because Jacob had loved Joseph more than all his brothers, and they hated him for that.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: Jacob saw that there was no water. He said that Unimbɔti had taken the water away.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: God is the one who created me and gave me birth, and I also gave birth to God. He did not create any other god.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.

translated: If you do not have a child, you are not a real woman, if you do not have a child, you are not a real man, if you do not have a child to carry on your name, you are not a real person, that is what our ancestors said.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.

translated: Abraham was a very old man, and the LORD had blessed him in every way. He had many sheep, cattle, and servants, and he was very rich.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: And it was told to Jacob that his son had slept with his wife, and Jacob was very angry because of the shameful thing that his son had done, and he said that his son had done a very shameful thing.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: Jacob has two sons. He said that Unimbɔti should give him the cow.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: God created man in his own image, in the image of God he created him. He created them male and female.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.

translated: My father had a cow that gave birth, the chief had a cow that gave birth, and the poor man also had a cow that gave birth, but my father's was the one that gave birth to a heifer.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.

translated: Abraham took his son, and two servants along with him, and a donkey loaded with wood for the burnt offering, and they went to the place that God had told him about.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.

translated: Jacob's sons became jealous of him because of the dreams he had, and because their father loved him more than all his brothers, and because of the dreams he had.

translated: Jacob was a very good man, and he had twelve sons who became the twelve tribes of Israel. He loved his son Joseph more than all his other sons because Joseph was born when Jacob was an old man.

ref: God blessed the seventh day and made it special, because on that day he rested from his work.
text: Míǹ puee Unimbɔti bīiǹ ŋwinluluútee pu ní ki gāntì-ŋu ki síiǹ u-ba, kun puee ŋwiin gbanti ní u kāl̀ ki ŋūŋfì atuun ŋin kɔkɔ u ŋáaǹee pu.
translated: When Unimbɔti was going to the farm, he met a man on the way, and when he was returning, he saw that the man had died and his body was lying on the road.

ref: The king told Joseph: I dreamed I was standing on the bank of the Nile River.
text: Ní Faaroo bí Jooseefi yii: «M dámintì yii m yú dimɔɔgbandi ní.
translated: I, Faroo, have begotten Jooseef. He is my beloved son.

ref: Deep water was still everywhere, and when the dove could not find a place to land, it flew back to the boat. Then Noah held out his hand and helped it back in.
text: Ama dibeenanjil kaa ká tin pu di làá tɔ́kɔńee ní ki fātìi nín gítí u-cee meela poon, kun puee nnyim nìn dáá gbíntìi bí kitiŋ kɔkɔ pu. Ní Noowa tāntì u-ŋal ki cúú-dì ki kɔ́ǹ u-cee meela poon.
translated: God loved the world so much that he gave his only begotten son so that whoever believes in him should not perish but have everlasting life. For God did not send his son into the world to condemn the world, but to save the world through him.

ref: Jacob's men took the gifts on ahead of him, but he spent the night in camp. Jacob's Name Is Changed to Israel
text: Ní ní kūtì kí lì yii: <A-tɔntɔnliu Jaakɔb u-ba páń boon ki dòoń.>» Di sá u lī u-neen yii: «M làá dū tiwaŋkuti nee kí pɔ̄-u kí sɔ̀ŋkì u-pɔbil, boonee ní kí dāá nín ká-u. An yaa yíì u táá sɔ̄ m-saai kí fōō-mi.»
translated: This is the story of Jacob: "Jacob was a man who herded his father's livestock." He said to his mother: "I will go to my brother so that he might bless me before he dies." But his mother said to him: "Go, my son. Do as I tell you."

ref: and forgets what you have done to him, I'll send for you to come home. Why should I lose both of my sons on the same day?”
text: U yaa láa súntì tin a ŋá-u u-ŋuul sɔ̄ŋkèe, m làá dāá tɔ̄ bí yíiń-si á gítiń doo. M yaa kaa ŋá míǹ, m làá dāá lóó ni-liitil nee ŋwimbaanti ní.»
translated: I was going to the farm to harvest my corn, but I saw a snake and ran away. I was so afraid, I saw my shadow and thought it was a ghost.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.

translated: God is the one who created me and gave me to my mother, and I was given to my mother by God. He is the one who knows my heart.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.

translated: Jacob was not the one who deceived him, but because Jacob was seeking the blessing that Isaac had intended for Esau, he received it instead, and that is why Esau did not get it.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
